panels.title: Monthly Revenue Trend
panels.chart_reasoning: A line chart is ideal for visualizing trends over time, making it suitable for showing the monthly revenue fluctuations.
panels.sql: SELECT STRFTIME('%Y-%m', Date) AS Month, ROUND(SUM(Total_Amount), 2) AS MonthlyRevenue FROM data_table GROUP BY Month ORDER BY Month;
panels.insight: Observe the overall upward or downward trend in monthly revenue and identify any seasonal peaks or dips.

panels.title: Revenue by Category
panels.chart_reasoning: A bar chart is effective for comparing the revenue generated by different categories, facilitating a clear understanding of which categories are performing best.

0053d2acae0efb021a60e1eac6947cf2.summary: Analysis of revenue by target audience, engagement score by customer segment, and acquisition cost by channel for campaigns.

panels.title: Revenue by Target Audience
panels.chart_reasoning: Bar chart is suitable for comparing a numerical metric (Revenue) across discrete categories (Target Audience).
panels.sql: SELECT Target_Audience, ROUND(SUM(Revenue), 2) AS TotalRevenue FROM data_table GROUP BY Target_Audience ORDER BY TotalRevenue DESC LIMIT 50
panels.insight: Premium Shoppers generate the highest revenue, indicating strong performance within this segment.

panels.title: Engagement Score by Customer Segment
panels.chart_reasoning: Bar chart is suitable for comparing a numerical metric (Engagement_Score) across discrete categories (Customer_Segment).
panels.sql: SELECT Customer_Segment, ROUND(AVG(Engagement_Score), 2) AS AverageEngagementScore FROM data_table GROUP BY Customer_Segment ORDER BY AverageEngagementScore DESC LIMIT 50
panels.insight: Working Women and Premium Shoppers exhibit the highest engagement scores, suggesting effective campaign resonance with these groups.

panels.title: Acquisition Cost by Channel
panels.chart_reasoning: Bar chart is suitable for comparing a numerical metric (Acquisition_Cost) across discrete categories (Channel_Used).
panels.sql: SELECT Channel_Used, ROUND(SUM(Acquisition_Cost), 2) AS TotalAcquisitionCost FROM data_table GROUP BY Channel_Used ORDER BY TotalAcquisitionCost DESC LIMIT 50
panels.insight: Google and Facebook represent the largest portions of acquisition costs, necessitating a review of their efficiency and ROI.

tldr.text: Premium Shoppers generate the highest revenue, indicating strong performance within this segment.
Working Women and Premium Shoppers exhibit the highest engagement scores, suggesting effective campaign resonance with these groups.
Google and Facebook represent the largest portions of acquisition costs, necessitating a review of their efficiency and ROI.

49ec34124e005b727fcfbb5be0cb773d.summary: Analyzing the effectiveness and cost of different marketing channels to understand their performance and optimize spending.

panels.title: Average Cost per Conversion by Channel
panels.chart_reasoning: This bar chart is suitable for comparing the average cost per conversion across different channels. It allows for easy identification of the most and least cost-effective channels.
panels.sql: SELECT Channel_Used, ROUND(SUM(Acquisition_Cost) / SUM(Conversions), 2) AS AvgCostPerConversion FROM data_table WHERE Conversions > 0 GROUP BY Channel_Used ORDER BY AvgCostPerConversion ASC LIMIT 10
panels.insight: Email and specific combinations of Email with other platforms appear to be the most cost-effective for conversions.

panels.title: Revenue Generated per Channel
panels.chart_reasoning: A bar chart is effective for comparing the total revenue generated by each marketing channel, allowing for a clear understanding of which channels drive the most value.
panels.sql: SELECT Channel_Used, ROUND(SUM(Revenue), 2) AS TotalRevenue FROM data_table GROUP BY Channel_Used ORDER BY TotalRevenue DESC LIMIT 10
panels.insight: Channels like 'Email, Facebook, Google, YouTube' and 'Email, Google, YouTube' are generating significant revenue, suggesting their high impact on top-line results.

panels.title: ROI by Channel
panels.chart_reasoning: This bar chart allows for a direct comparison of the Return on Investment (ROI) across various channels, highlighting which channels are most profitable.
panels.sql: SELECT Channel_Used, ROUND(SUM(ROI), 2) AS TotalROI FROM data_table GROUP BY Channel_Used ORDER BY TotalROI DESC LIMIT 10
panels.insight: Channels such as 'WhatsApp, Google, YouTube' and 'Email, Google, WhatsApp' show strong positive ROI, indicating efficient use of budget for profit generation.

tldr.text: Email and specific combinations of Email with other platforms appear to be the most cost-effective for conversions.
Channels like 'Email, Facebook, Google, YouTube' and 'Email, Google, YouTube' are generating significant revenue, suggesting their high impact on top-line results.
Channels such as 'WhatsApp, Google, YouTube' and 'Email, Google, WhatsApp' show strong positive ROI, indicating efficient use of budget for profit generation.
